title: chiococca alba var. parvifolia
intro: Chiococca alba var. parvifolia, also known as the small-leaved yellow jessamine, is a type of flowering vine native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and pneumonia. The health benefits of Chiococca alba var. parvifolia include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce the risk of heart disease, and act as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Chiococca alba var. parvifolia is typically prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and can be used to make herbal teas, salves, and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
